title: Training Methodology Candidates To Add
description: Candidates for the human-enablement dimension of the catalog: methodologies for training PEOPLE to use, automate with, build with, and build on AI/agents — distinct from the methodologies collection, which is about constructing AI SYSTEMS. Each candidate is a teachable Move (or Track) located on the Craft Path, with real-world Known Uses as evidence. Entries graduate by being authored into a training-src/ collection and removed from this list. The craft lexicon (Craft Path / Step / Move / Track / Threshold / Masterpiece / Craft / Container / Mode / Persona / Reach / Guardrail / Acculturation) is the single source of truth, captured under `taxonomy`.

taxonomy.description: The shared backbone. The Craft Path (ordered spine) plus the orthogonal facets every Move is located against.

id.description: Proposed kebab-case id. Must not collide with any existing catalog id.

name.description: Craft Move name in our lexicon (e.g. 'Build Sprint', 'Practice Guild', 'Acculturation').

aliases.description: Names this idea goes by in the wild, including original-language terms (e.g. 'AI hackathon', '虞の穴', 'acculturation IA').

kind.description: foundation = pre-Operator culture layer; move = atomic teachable unit; track = orchestrated multi-Step program; guardrail = cross-cutting safety lens.

step.description: Which Step(s) of the Craft Path this serves.

container.description: workshop | build-clinic | bootcamp | cohort-course | hackathon | dojo | leadership-retreat | coaching | async | embedded

mode.description: concept | hands-on-build | byo-problem | mentored-unblock | pair-cohort | capstone

persona.description: non-technical | analyst-ops | manager-leader | builder | founder | learner

craft.description: AI Fluency 4D: delegation | description | discernment | diligence; plus literacy-basics

summary.description: One or two sentences in plain, simple English: what the Move does and when it pays off.

mechanics.description: Short bullet steps describing how the session actually runs.

proof.description: The Masterpiece / measurable outcome that shows the Step was earned (e.g. 'shipped one working tool', '10-15% productivity uplift').

ecosystem.description: Where the idea is observed: anthropic | openai | google | microsoft | aws | nvidia | salesforce | consultancy | national | neutral | in-house.

origin_lang.description: If surfaced via a non-English scan, the source language (e.g. 'ja', 'zh', 'fr', 'de', 'ko', 'es', 'ar').

known_uses.description: Real-world programs that exemplify this Move. The catalog's known_uses role — official courses and company experiments enter HERE, not as patterns of their own.

quote.description: Short verbatim excerpt (<= ~300 chars) supporting the claim.

maturity.description: proven = used repeatedly across many orgs; emerging = leading practitioners; experimental = single-org or research-stage.

verification_status.description: Honest grading of how well the entry traces to cited sources. Soft-passes are not acceptable.

status.description: Lifecycle: proposed = on the list; drafting = being authored; authored = lives in training-src/; rejected = considered and dropped.

related.description: Other candidate ids that combine with, precede, succeed, or contrast this one.
